events.label: reasoning
events.text: We need answer JSON only. Need assess scenario: heavy industrial structural ironwork, column/beam erection & plumb, rough-complete. Observed condition: temporary bracing removed before decking/permanent stability. This is a defect and safety hazard. According to AISC 303 and OSHA 1926 Subpart R, temporary bracing must remain until structure is permanently stabilized/decking installed. Removing bracing prematurely can cause collapse. Decision fail. Risk high or critical? Temporary bracing removed before permanent stability is critical because potential collapse. But maybe high. Need decide. In structural erection, premature removal of bracing is a leading cause of collapse; OSHA requires stability maintained at all times. I'd rate critical due to imminent collapse hazard. Work_stage rough-complete. Component_conditions installed-defective, non-compliant. Percent complete: treat as roughly 70% complete until defects corrected. So percent_complete 70. Value 0. Sound_source irrelevant empty? Schema string; can use "". confidence 1.0. event_time 0, rate 0, order empty, workable empty. Findings: temporary bracing removed before decking/permanent stability; structure not permanently stabilized; potential for column/beam instability or collapse. Actions: immediately reinstall/restore temporary bracing per erection plan; prohibit work beneath/around; inspect for displacement/damage; engineer verification before further decking; re-plumb and align as needed; do not remove bracing until permanent stability achieved per AISC/OSHA. Rationale: premature removal violates OSHA 1926 Subpart R and AISC 303 stability requirements; creates critical collapse hazard; work cannot pass until corrected. References: AISC 303, OSHA 1926 Subpart R. Need ensure JSON valid. No prose outside.

    "Temporary bracing has been removed before decking/permanent stability was achieved.",
    "Columns and beams may be unstable and susceptible to displacement or collapse.",
    "Condition violates structural stability requirements for erection."
  ],
  "actions": [
    "Immediately reinstall temporary bracing per the approved erection plan and engineer's requirements.",
    "Restrict access to the area and prohibit work below or adjacent to the unstable steel until stability is restored.",
    "Inspect all erected columns and beams for plumb, alignment, and damage caused by premature bracing removal.",
    "Obtain engineer verification that permanent stability is achieved before any further bracing is removed.",
    "Do not proceed with decking or close-out until compliance is confirmed."
  ],
  "rationale": "Removing temporary bracing before the structure has permanent stability creates an imminent collapse hazard and violates AISC 303 and OSHA 1926 Subpart R requirements to maintain stability during erection. The work is therefore failed and must be corrected before progress can resume.",

    "AISC 303",
    "OSHA 1926 Subpart R"
  ]
